page: Microsoft Azure L16s is a storage-optimized virtual machine belonging to the LS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. This configuration provides 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz, supported by a 40 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage-intensive tasks, it bundles 2874 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPU hardware is included.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% respectively, reflecting the older CPU architecture and modest clock speed. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, reach average performance tiers. Memory bandwidth presents a similar tradeoff: while cached small-block read operations perform at average levels, uncached large-block write bandwidth is poor, placing in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L16s offers high resource density, particularly regarding its large local storage capacity and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a viable option for workloads that do not require the latest generation compute speeds. It is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression tasks, static web serving, and Redis deployments, where its average multi-core capabilities and substantial local storage can be fully leveraged.
description: Microsoft Azure L16s is an x86_64 storage-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 2874 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. While single-core CPU benchmarks and large-block memory write bandwidth fall into lower performance tiers, its multi-core performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis operations consistently achieve average benchmark ratings. This hardware profile offers a qualitative balance of high local storage capacity and memory density relative to its compute generation. The L16s is suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and memory-intensive workloads that benefit from large local storage without requiring cutting-edge single-thread processing speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16s is a storage-optimized VM featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2874 GB local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for database and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16s features 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2874 GB local storage, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and balanced memory density for database and multi-threaded workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
2874 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Suited for database operations, Redis, and static web serving
